title: Gay culture and leisure group FLay forms in Schaan, Liechtenstein
description: In February 1998 the organization FLay was set up in Schaan as a culture and leisure association for homosexual people in Liechtenstein.

title: National helpline Rainbow Line launches in Switzerland
description: From 1 March 1998 Pink Cross, together with the regional groups HAZ, HABS, HAB and HALU and the Zurich lesbian counseling service, operated Rainbow Line, a Switzerland-wide information and counseling phone service that also collected reports of homophobic violence against individuals and groups.

title: Pink Apple film festival debuts in Frauenfeld
description: The first edition of Pink Apple, a gay and lesbian film festival in the canton of Thurgau, ran in Frauenfeld from 8 May to 29 June 1998 and became an annual event.

title: Lesbian & Gay Pride held in Lausanne
description: A Lesbian & Gay Pride event took place in Lausanne on 4 July 1998, part of the annual pride gatherings of French-speaking Switzerland.

title: Pink Cross opens French-language office in Geneva
description: In July 1998 the national gay organization Pink Cross opened a Secrétariat romand in Geneva to serve French-speaking Switzerland.

title: Zurich CSD returns to political speeches and awards its first prize
description: The Christopher Street Day parade in Zurich on 18 July 1998 again featured political speeches and slogans, and for the first time a publicly known person received the CSD award.

title: Alarm-clock protest at the Federal Palace in Bern
description: Activists staged an alarm-clock action outside the Federal Palace on 17 August 1998, demanding an answer at last to the petition for equal rights for same-sex couples.

title: Zurich voters approve city funding for sex-worker service Hermann
description: In a municipal ballot on 28 and 29 November 1998, Zurich voters granted 30,000 francs to Hermann, a counseling service for sex workers, clearly defeating the SVP campaign against the subsidy.

title: Parliamentary initiative filed to register cohabiting couples
description: Geneva National Councillor Jean-Michel Gros of the liberal party submitted a parliamentary initiative on 30 November 1998 calling for the legal registration of cohabiting couples, an early step toward Swiss partnership legislation.

title: Parliamentary initiative filed for same-sex marriage in Switzerland
description: Zurich National Councillor Ruth Genner of the Green Party submitted a parliamentary initiative on 18 December 1998 seeking changes to the Swiss civil code, citizenship law and civil-status ordinance so that marriage between same-sex couples would be regulated.

title: LGBT association 360° forms in Geneva
description: The Geneva-based association 360° was created in 1998 as a lesbian, gay, bisexual and transgender organization and began publishing the magazine 360° for French-speaking Switzerland.

title: VoGay opens the InfoBi counseling service in Lausanne
description: In 1998 the Vaud organization VoGay began offering counseling in Lausanne through its InfoBi service.

title: Regional queer conference launches in central Switzerland
description: A joint lesbian, bisexual and gay conference for the central Swiss cantons was brought to life in 1998, connecting community groups across the region.

title: Winterthur group Wisch gets its own club venue
description: In 1998 the Winterthur organization Wisch obtained a club space of its own, giving the local community a regular meeting place.

title: New Swiss constitution bans discrimination based on way of life
description: On 18 April 1999 Swiss voters approved the new federal constitution, whose Article 8 paragraph 2 forbids discrimination on the grounds of a person's way of life — wording understood to cover sexual orientation.

title: Der Kreis exhibition shown in Berlin
description: From 21 April to 25 July 1999, Berlin's gay museum presented an exhibition on the Zurich circle Der Kreis, covering its members, artists and authors.

title: Bern newsletter gayAgenda begins regular publication
description: From May 1999 the gayAgenda, the bulletin of the Bern homosexual working groups (HAB), started appearing on a regular schedule.

title: Zurich CSD marks thirty years since Stonewall
description: The Christopher Street Day parade in Zurich on 26 June 1999 took the thirtieth anniversary of the Stonewall uprising as its theme, coupling it with a demand for equal rights now.

title: Romandie pride gathers in Fribourg
description: The Lesbian & Gay Pride of French-speaking Switzerland was hosted by Fribourg from 2 to 4 July 1999.

title: Six thousand march in Bern for a partnership law
description: On 18 September 1999 about 6,000 lesbians and gay men demonstrated in Bern under a slogan of equal love and equal rights, backing National Councillor Jean-Michel Gros's parliamentary initiative for a registered partnership law.

title: National Council orders drafting of a partnership law
description: On 27 September 1999 the Swiss National Council instructed its legal affairs committee to draw up legislation on registered partnership for same-sex couples.

title: Ecumenical group HuK Switzerland dissolves
description: The national organization Homosexuals and Church (HuK) Switzerland disbanded on 13 November 1999; its Zurich branch carried on until 2004.

title: Pink Cross starts a youth and schools working group
description: In 1999 Pink Cross created a specialist group on youth and school issues, followed a year later by the complementary school project GLL on same-sex love.

title: Association Juragai forms in Delémont
description: Juragai, a mixed homosexual association serving the Jura Arc region, was established in Delémont in 1999.

title: School education project ABQ starts in canton Bern
description: The ABQ school project, an association bringing education about same-sex love into classrooms, came into being in the canton of Bern in 1999.

title: First Queer Sunday film screenings in St. Gallen
description: St. Gallen held its first Queer Sunday in 1999, a film event that went on to recur in the city.

title: Social Democrats create a gay and lesbian commission
description: On 4 March 2000 the Social Democratic Party of Switzerland set up a specialist commission for gay men and lesbians within the party.

title: Der Kreis exhibition shown at the Swiss National Museum
description: The exhibition on Der Kreis and its members, artists and authors ran from 2 May to 25 June 2000 at the Swiss national museum in Zurich, then called the Landesmuseum.

title: Zurich hosts the EuroGames 2000
description: The EuroGames, a European sports event for queer people, took place in Zurich from 1 to 4 June 2000, framed by a cultural program named Warmer Mai. The organizers brought the Thurgau film festival Pink Apple to Zurich for the occasion, and it afterwards ran annually in both cities.

title: Zurich CSD keeps up the push for equal rights
description: The Christopher Street Day parade in Zurich on 24 June 2000 pressed the demand for equal rights, with a motto naming lesbian, gay, bisexual and binational concerns.

title: Bern pride highlights binational couples
description: The Lesbian & Gay Pride in Bern on 8 July 2000 ran under a motto of love without borders, calling for better legal standing for life partners holding foreign passports.

title: Cultural association Warmer Mai forms in Zurich
description: On 6 September 2000 the association Warmer Mai was set up in Zurich; it went on to assemble a queer cultural program every May.

title: Pink Cross and LOS establish the Fonds Respect
description: On 18 October 2000 the national organizations Pink Cross and LOS jointly created Respect, a lesbian and gay fund usually known as the Fonds Respect.

title: Federal Council commits to legal recognition of same-sex partnerships
description: On 25 October 2000 Federal Councillor Ruth Metzler-Arnold, head of the federal justice and police department, announced the Federal Council's decision in principle that same-sex partnerships should receive legal recognition and state protection, with the government to draft a corresponding law.

title: Symposium on partnership law held in Zurich
description: On 4 November 2000 the association network convened a symposium at a Zurich hotel on the legal regulation of same-sex partnerships in Europe, with participants from all major Swiss parties and speakers from Switzerland and five other European states.

title: European umbrella body for gay managers forms
description: The European Gay Manager Association (EGMA) was created in 2000 as an international umbrella organization for European associations of gay professionals; founding members included network from Switzerland, the Völklinger Kreis from Germany and L'Autre Cercle from France.

title: School project GLL begins offering classroom lessons
description: In 2000 Pink Cross, LOS and Fels jointly set up GLL, a school project whose teams offered lessons about homosexuality at schools in several regions of Switzerland.

title: Pink Cross starts its religion working group
description: The working group Plattform Religion came into being within Pink Cross in 2000 to address questions of religion and homosexuality.

title: Gay TV-listings magazine GayTV launches
description: GayTV, the first gay and lesbian television guide in Switzerland, was launched in 2000 and subsequently appeared every two weeks.

title: Housing association Andersheim forms in Zurich
description: Andersheim, an association promoting a new gay and lesbian form of living aimed mainly at older people, was created in Zurich in 2000 to develop living arrangements offering homosexual men and women a protected yet open setting for everyday life.

title: Queer community group forms in canton Uri
description: On 21 January 2001 a lesbian, bisexual and gay group — its name a colloquial shrug meaning "so what?" — came together in the canton of Uri and published its own newsletter titled Information.

title: Geneva introduces the first cantonal partnership registration in Switzerland
description: On 15 February 2001 the cantonal partnership scheme PACS took effect in Geneva, the first law in any Swiss canton allowing same-sex couples to register their partnership.

title: First same-sex couples register in Geneva
description: On 8 May 2001 the first same-sex couples in the canton of Geneva — the first anywhere in Switzerland — had their partnerships officially registered under the new cantonal scheme.

title: Federal president addresses Zurich CSD
description: The Christopher Street Day parade in Zurich on 23 June 2001 focused on discrimination in the workplace, and Federal President Moritz Leuenberger gave a speech.

title: National pride gathering held in Sion
description: The Lesbian & Gay Pride took place in Sion, canton of Valais, on 7 and 8 July 2001 as a national gathering.

title: First encounter day of Swiss queer groups
description: On 8 September 2001 gay and lesbian groups held their first joint day of encounters, featuring a hike through the Töss valley and a guided tour of composer Paul Burkhard's house and museum in Zell.

title: First Swiss brochure on queer people at work published
description: On 9 October 2001 the workplace specialist group of the Swiss lesbian and gay organizations issued Queer im Job, the first brochure on the situation of queer people in the working world.

title: Draft federal partnership law presented
description: On 14 November 2001 Federal Councillor Ruth Metzler presented the preliminary draft of a federal law on same-sex partnership.

title: Lesbian businesswomen's network WyberNet forms
description: WyberNet gay business women, a network for lesbian professionals in business, was established in Switzerland in 2001.

title: Queer group Pink Cevi starts within Swiss YMCA and YWCA circles
description: Pink Cevi, a group for members of the Christian associations of young men (CVJM) and young women (CVJF), came into being in 2001.
